questionText: What instrument did Thelonious Monk play?
funFact: Monk had a habit of standing up mid-performance and dancing in circles around the piano — his eccentric behavior was part of his genius

questionText: Which country is Chopin from?
funFact: Chopin's heart was literally smuggled out of France in a jar of cognac after his death — it's preserved in a church pillar in Warsaw to this day

questionText: Who founded Depeche Mode?
correctAnswer: Vince Clarke (who then left to form Erasure)
options: Vince Clarke (who then left to form Erasure)
funFact: Vince Clarke left after ONE album and formed Yazoo, then Erasure — Martin Gore took over songwriting and Depeche Mode became much darker and more successful without him

questionText: Which animated film features 'Into the Unknown' by Idina Menzel?
funFact: 'Into the Unknown' was nominated for the Oscar but lost to '(I'm Gonna) Love Me Again' by Elton John — Frozen fans were outraged

questionText: Which TV series features 'My Way' by Frank Sinatra reinterpreted in its finale?
correctAnswer: The Sopranos (cut to black during 'Don't Stop Believin')
options: The Sopranos (cut to black during 'Don't Stop Believin')
funFact: The Sopranos finale cut to black mid-song during Journey's 'Don't Stop Believin'' — it's the most debated ending in TV history. Creator David Chase has never fully explained it

questionText: What record label did Dr. Dre co-found with Jimmy Iovine?
correctAnswer: Beats Electronics (not a label — Aftermath is his label)
options: Beats Electronics (not a label — Aftermath is his label)
funFact: Dr. Dre sold Beats to Apple for $3 billion in 2014 — making him briefly the richest person in hip-hop history from selling headphones, not music

questionText: Which country is Sepultura from?
funFact: Sepultura means 'grave' in Portuguese — they formed in Belo Horizonte in 1984 and became the most successful metal band from South America

questionText: Who is the lead singer of Mew?
funFact: Mew's 'Frengers' album title is a combination of 'friends' and 'strangers' — their art-rock sound made them one of Denmark's most unique musical exports

questionText: Which Denis Villeneuve film features Jóhann Jóhannsson's eerie score with reversed sounds?
funFact: Jóhannsson's Arrival score uses human voices processed until they sound alien — reflecting the film's theme that language shapes reality

questionText: Which TV series about a chemistry teacher turned drug lord features 'Baby Blue' by Badfinger in its finale?
funFact: 'Baby Blue' by Badfinger was a deep cut from 1972 — after Breaking Bad's finale aired, it re-entered the iTunes Top 10 within hours

questionText: What was Otis Redding's last recording before his death?
correctAnswer: (Sittin' On) The Dock of the Bay
options: (Sittin' On) The Dock of the Bay
funFact: Otis Redding recorded 'Dock of the Bay' just three days before dying in a plane crash at age 26 — the whistling at the end was a placeholder he planned to replace with lyrics
backgroundSongName: (Sittin' On) The Dock of the Bay

questionText: Which country is José González from?
funFact: José González has Argentine parents but was born and raised in Gothenburg, Sweden — his cover of 'Heartbeats' for a Sony Bravia bouncing balls commercial made him globally famous

questionText: Who is the lead singer of Weezer?
funFact: Rivers Cuomo went to Harvard between Weezer albums — he studied English literature while 'Pinkerton' was being panned by critics (it's now considered a masterpiece)

questionText: Which animated film features 'We Don't Talk About Bruno'?
funFact: 'We Don't Talk About Bruno' spent 5 weeks at #1 — making it the first Disney song to top the Billboard Hot 100 since 'A Whole New World' in 1993

questionText: Which TV show features 'Love Is the Drug' by Roxy Music?
funFact: Bryan Ferry's suave persona made Roxy Music perfect for a show about plastic surgery — 'Love Is the Drug' captures superficial beauty culture perfectly

questionText: Which pianist composed 'Clair de Lune'?
funFact: 'Clair de Lune' means 'Moonlight' in French — it's been used in over 50 films and TV shows, from Ocean's Eleven to Twilight

questionText: Which country is Travis Scott from?
funFact: Travis Scott's real name is Jacques Berman Webster II — he took his stage name from a favorite uncle named Travis and Kid Cudi's first name Scott

questionText: Who is the lead singer of New Order?
funFact: New Order formed from the ashes of Joy Division after Ian Curtis' death — they reinvented themselves with electronic dance music and 'Blue Monday' became the best-selling 12-inch single ever

questionText: Which coming-of-age film features 'Asleep' by The Smiths?
correctAnswer: The Perks of Being a Wallflower
options: The Perks of Being a Wallflower
funFact: The Perks of Being a Wallflower captures how discovering The Smiths as a teenager feels like finding a secret language — author Stephen Chbosky wrote the book partly as a love letter to 1980s indie music

questionText: Which TV series about a paper company features a theme by Jay Ferguson?
funFact: The Office theme was inspired by an English folk melody — Jay Ferguson recorded it in his garage. It's been covered by more YouTube musicians than almost any other TV theme

questionText: What was Tupac's only #1 hit during his lifetime?
funFact: 'Changes' — Tupac's most famous song — was released posthumously in 1998, two years after his death. He recorded it in one take in 1992

questionText: Which country is King Diamond from?
funFact: King Diamond (Kim Bendix Petersen) paints his face like a corpse and sings in a four-octave falsetto — Metallica's Lars Ulrich calls him 'the greatest metal vocalist ever'

questionText: Who plays bass in Tool?
funFact: Tool's albums use mathematical sequences like the Fibonacci sequence in their time signatures — Danny Carey's drum patterns are studied in university music programs

questionText: Which Bond film features Sam Smith's 'Writing's on the Wall'?
funFact: Sam Smith wrote 'Writing's on the Wall' in 20 minutes and cried while recording it — it became the first Bond theme to reach #1 in the UK since 'A View to a Kill' in 1985

questionText: Which animated series features a theme by The Ramones?
correctAnswer: Spider-Man: The Animated Series (1994) — though the Ramones covered the 1967 theme separately
options: Spider-Man: The Animated Series (1994) — though the Ramones covered the 1967 theme separately
funFact: The Ramones' cover of the Spider-Man theme captures punk's love of pop culture — they turned a children's TV theme into a 90-second blast of pure energy

questionText: Which rapper wrote the bestselling poetry book 'The Rose That Grew from Concrete'?
funFact: Tupac wrote poetry throughout his life — 'The Rose That Grew from Concrete' was published posthumously and revealed a deeply sensitive artist behind the gangsta rap persona

questionText: Which country is Lil Nas X from?
funFact: Lil Nas X bought the beat for 'Old Town Road' for $30 online — the song spent 19 weeks at #1 and was initially removed from Billboard's country chart, sparking a genre debate

questionText: Who is the vocalist of Evanescence?
funFact: Amy Lee classically trained in piano from age 9 — 'Bring Me to Life' blended gothic metal with piano ballad, selling over 17 million copies of 'Fallen'

questionText: Which Stanley Kubrick film features Beethoven's 9th Symphony prominently?
funFact: Kubrick's use of 'Ode to Joy' during scenes of violence was deeply controversial — it permanently changed how audiences perceive Beethoven's most joyful composition

questionText: Which crime series features a reggae-influenced theme called 'Magnum P.I.'?
funFact: Mike Post composed themes for more hit TV shows than anyone in history — Magnum, Law & Order, NYPD Blue, The A-Team, and many more

questionText: What was the first rap song to reach #1 on the Billboard Hot 100?
funFact: Debbie Harry name-checked Grandmaster Flash and Fab Five Freddy in 'Rapture' — bringing hip-hop culture to mainstream pop audiences for the first time

questionText: Which country is Rosalía from?
funFact: Rosalía's 'MOTOMAMI' was named the best album of 2022 by multiple critics — she fuses 800-year-old flamenco tradition with reggaeton and hyperpop

questionText: Who was the drummer of Nirvana before Dave Grohl?
funFact: Chad Channing played on Nirvana's debut 'Bleach' — when Dave Grohl replaced him, the band's sound became dramatically heavier, leading directly to 'Nevermind'

questionText: Which film features 'Time After Time' by Cyndi Lauper?
options: Romy and Michele's High School Reunion
funFact: 'Time After Time' was so universally loved that Miles Davis covered it — one of the only pop songs the jazz legend ever recorded

questionText: Which TV series features the theme 'Concerning Hobbits' style pastoral music by Howard Shore?
correctAnswer: The Lord of the Rings: The Rings of Power
options: The Lord of the Rings: The Rings of Power
funFact: Howard Shore composed over 12 hours of music for the original Lord of the Rings trilogy — it's the largest orchestral film score ever recorded

questionText: Which Danish artist had the international hit 'Move Your Feet'?
funFact: Junior Senior's pixel-art music video for 'Move Your Feet' was nominated for a Grammy — the song's infectious energy made it a global club anthem in 2003

questionText: Which country is WhoMadeWho from?
funFact: WhoMadeWho went from Copenhagen indie to headlining Burning Man and Tale of Us' Afterlife events — becoming one of the most in-demand live electronic acts globally

questionText: Who is the lead singer of L.O.C. (Lansen Over Cph)?
funFact: L.O.C. is one of Denmark's most successful rappers — his album 'Prespp' went diamond in Denmark and he performed at Roskilde Festival multiple times

questionText: Which Marvel film features 'Surrender' by Cheap Trick?
funFact: James Gunn's Guardians trilogy is essentially a love letter to 1970s-80s rock — the 'Awesome Mixes' soundtracks outsold most original scores

questionText: Which TV series features David Bowie's 'Lazarus' in its final season?
correctAnswer: The Leftovers (uses 'Let the Mystery Be' but featured Bowie's music)
options: The Leftovers (uses 'Let the Mystery Be' but featured Bowie's music)
funFact: 'Lazarus' was recorded just days before Bowie's death — the lyrics 'Look up here, I'm in heaven' were his deliberate farewell to the world

questionText: What is the best-selling album of all time?
options: The Dark Side of the Moon by Pink Floyd
funFact: Thriller has sold over 70 million copies — every single one of its 9 tracks was released as a single, and 7 reached the top 10. No album will ever match it

questionText: Which country is Carpark North from?
funFact: Carpark North's 'Human' was used in the FIFA video game series — bringing Danish electronic rock to gamers worldwide

questionText: Who is the vocalist of Nine Inch Nails?
funFact: Trent Reznor recorded 'The Downward Spiral' in the house where the Manson family murdered Sharon Tate — he moved out after discovering this, calling it 'a terrible coincidence'

questionText: Which horror film franchise features 'Tubular Bells' by Mike Oldfield?
funFact: Mike Oldfield was only 19 when he recorded 'Tubular Bells' — he played over 20 instruments himself. Its use in The Exorcist made it one of the most recognizable pieces of music ever

questionText: Which Danish-Swedish crime series features 'Hollow Talk' by Choir of Young Believers?
funFact: 'Hollow Talk' went from obscure Copenhagen indie track to internationally recognized theme — it was played at Nordic noir conventions and film festivals worldwide
